steps.title: Square to make a quadratic
steps.content: The equation is $$\sqrt{k-x}=32-x.$$ Since the left side is nonnegative, any real solution must have $32-x\ge 0$, or $x\le 32$. Squaring gives $$k-x=(32-x)^2.$$

steps.title: Put the quadratic in standard form
steps.content: Expand and move everything to one side: $$k-x=x^2-64x+1024$$ $$0=x^2-63x+1024-k.$$

steps.title: Force exactly one real solution
steps.content: For the quadratic to have exactly one real solution, set the discriminant equal to $0$: $$63^2-4(1)(1024-k)=0$$ $$3969-4096+4k=0$$ $$4k=127.$$

steps.title: Check the domain condition
steps.content: The repeated root is $x=\frac{63}{2}=31.5$, and $31.5\le 32$, so it satisfies the original radical equation's requirement. Therefore the minimum possible value of $4k$ is 127.
